ddls:
CREATE TABLE "test_cases" (
  "test_case_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each test case */,
  "test_suite_id" INTEGER /* Identifier for the associated test suite */,
  "test_type" TEXT /* Type of the test case (e.g., functional, integration, unit testing) */,
  "action" TEXT /* Action being tested (e.g., Login, Authenticated) */,
  "input" TEXT /* Input or trigger for the test case (e.g., Login Server on Https.) */,
  "expected_result" TEXT /* Expected result of the test case (e.g., Login.html page) */,
  "actual_result" TEXT /* Actual result of the test case (e.g., Login.html page) */,
  "is_valid" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the test case result is valid (Y/N) */,
  "assigned_to" INTEGER /* Identifier for the tester assigned to the test case */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the test case (e.g., Not Started, In Progress, Passed, Failed, Blocked) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the test case was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the test case */,
  "version" INTEGER /* Version number of the test case */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the test case */,
  "priority" TEXT /* Priority of the test case (e.g., Low, Medium, High) */,
  "execution_time" TEXT /* Timestamp when the test case was executed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("test_case_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "test_suites" (
  "test_suite_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each test suite */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the test suite */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the test suite */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the test suite was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the test suite */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("test_suite_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "testers" (
  "tester_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each tester */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the tester */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the tester */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the tester (e.g., QA Engineer, QA Lead) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the tester was added */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the tester */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tester_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "test_results" (
  "result_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each test result */,
  "test_case_id" INTEGER /* Identifier for the associated test case */,
  "execution_time" TEXT /* Timestamp when the test was executed */,
  "status" TEXT /* Result status (e.g., Passed, Failed, Blocked) */,
  "actual_result" TEXT /* Actual result of the test case */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the result was recorded */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the result */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("result_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the role (e.g., Admin, Tester, Viewer) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* List of permissions associated with the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_role_assignments" (
  "assignment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role assignment */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Identifier for the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Identifier for the role */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the role was assigned */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the role assignment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("assignment_id")
)
